title: Turr Phennir
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|a military officer of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and its successor states|other individuals named [[Phennir (disambiguation)]]}} {{Character |type=Confederation |image=[[File:TurrPhennir_XWing25.jpg]] |name=Turr Phennir |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 22]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 23]] |pronouns=He/him{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Major Turr Phennir}} |height=Below average''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]'' |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blond]] |feathers= |eyes=Dark brown{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other}}Game Update 20: Galactic Civil War 2 - Space |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Phennir (Count)|Count Phennir]] {{C|adoptive}}{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-10-the-rise-of-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 10: The Rise of the Empire}} |partners= |siblings=[[Cartha]] |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Imperial Remnant]] *[[Confederation]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Another day, another chance to kill Rebels.|Turr Phennir|}} '''Turr Phennir''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] hailing from the [[Valahari sector/Legends|Valahari Provinces]] who served for [[Standard year/Legends|years]] as an [[Ace/Legends|ace]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]]. Recruited into the group by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Phennir spent the next several years [[Starfighter combat/Legends|battling]] forces of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the Empire's opponent in the ongoing [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], he was a major and the fighter group's [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]]. Phennir and the 181st [[Space warfare/Legends|flew]] in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] that year, which saw [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[Death/Legends|die]] aboard the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. The defeat caused the Empire to [[Imperial Civil War|fracture]], but the 181st remained loyal to the throne and were called on to [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|defend]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] from the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the Alliance's successor state. Phennir had no faith in the Empire's new [[Sate Pestage/Legends|leadership]] and urged Fel to seize the planet as his own [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] holding, but the 181st instead flew into a battle that scheming [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Ysanne Isard]] had already decided they would lose. When the New Republic conquered Brentaal IV and captured Fel, command of the 181st was given to Phennir. When the [[Crimson Empire|last vestige]] of the Empire [[Battle of Ord Cantrell|ceased to exist]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the [[Imperial Remnant]] was [[Imperial Reunification|formed]] from the holdings of various warlords, and new [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] recruited Phennir and the 181st to aid him in the [[Orinda campaign]] of [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]. The fighter group and the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']] battled [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic forces]] in a series of clashes throughout the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], ultimately [[Battle of Orinda|repelling]] them in the [[Orinda system/Legends|Orinda system]] and securing the Remnant's borders. [[13 ABY/Legends|One year later]], [[General/Legends|General]] Phennir and [[Unidentified 181st pilot|three]] [[Unidentified 181st pilot 2|other]] [[Unidentified 181st pilot 3|pilots]] of the 181st went on a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission to the neutral planet [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]], whose government also invited representatives from the New Republic. Phennir and his men won the [[Heart/Legends|hearts]] of the locals by frequently engaging them in customary aerial duels to the death, and they pledged themselves to the nation state of [[Cartann]] when its [[Pekaelic ke Teldan|leader]] declared [[Adumari Civil War|war]] on the rest of the planet. However, Phennir fled Adumar in his [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptor]] when the New Republic pilots—flying for the opposing [[Adumari Union]]—shot down all three of his fellows. Cartann lost the war, and Adumar joined the New Republic. After Adumar, Phennir remained out of action for years, resurfacing in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] to join a [[Confederation]] of planets that had seceded from the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], the successor to the New Republic. As the [[Supreme Commander of the Confederation military]], he led their [[Second Galactic Civil War|war]] effort against the Alliance. His first action was to lure enemy [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet|forces]] into a [[Battle of Gilatter VIII|trap]] at the planet [[Gilatter VIII]], where [[Mine/Legends|mines]] awaited their [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. Phennir later quarreled with [[Sadras Koyan]], [[Five World Prime Minister|Prime Minister]] of the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system's]] [[Five Worlds]] government. Although the Five Worlds were members of the Confederation, Koyan independently [[Battle of the rendezvous point|used]] their [[Centerpoint Station|Centerpoint]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] against the Alliance, and Phennir responded by cutting the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] off from Confederate aid until Koyan was [[Battle of Centerpoint Station (Second Galactic Civil War)|removed]] from power by his [[Minister of Information (Five Worlds)|Minister of Information]], [[Denjax Teppler]]. As the war drew to a close in [[41 ABY]], the Confederation [[Battle of Uroro Station|surrendered]] when the Alliance joined forces with the Imperial Remnant and the [[Jedi Coalition]]. Phennir remained the leader of the independent government and represented them at a [[Unification Summit]] held on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[43 ABY]]. ==Biography== ===Rising in the ranks=== ====Joining the 181st==== {{Quote|Glad to have you with us, Captain Phennir.|Soontir Fel welcomes Turr Phennir into the 181st Imperial Fighter Group|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]]}} [[File:Forthegloryoftheempire.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Turr Phennir is accepted into the 181st.]] Turr Phennir was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was born in the [[Valahari sector/Legends|Valahari Provinces]] and raised by his [[Parent/Legends|stepfather]], the renowned hunting [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Phennir (Count)|Phennir]]. While the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] raged between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], both Phennir and his brother [[Cartha|"Chaser" Cartha]] served the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] as [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] pilots, but Cartha was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in action during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' by [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Phennir continued to serve, and by approximately [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],[[Soontir Fel]] [[Marriage/Legends|proposes]] to [[Syal Antilles Fel|Wynssa Starflare]] in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]] "nearly a year" after first meeting her shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Phennir is shown joining the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]] between these events. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the Battle of Yavin in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Phennir must have joined the 181st by around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. he had achieved the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and received a scar on the left side of his face. At that [[Time/Legends|time]], he was recruited into the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]] by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] on the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Phennir had turned down an offer to join the Imperial [[Black Squadron (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Black Squadron]] in order to get into the 181st. Fel had recently transformed the 181st from a [[Fighter wing/Legends|fighter wing]] into an elite three–[[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] fighter group, and he saw in Phennir a genuine desire to further the glory of the Empire. Although the 181st was officially led by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Evir Derricote]], Fel took charge of the group's training and spent the next two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] preparing his [[TIE pilot|pilots]] for a variety of missions. Flying [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], Phennir and his fellows [[Starfighter combat/Legends|engaged]] and destroyed Rebel forces on numerous occasions. ====Fame and glory==== {{Gamemechanics}} At some point after the Battle of Yavin, Phennir encountered a lone Rebel pilot somewhere in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. A dogfight ensued, and although Phennir was bested, he escaped in his damaged TIE fighter. Around the same time, Phennir participated in several [[Space warfare/Legends|space battles]] between Imperial and Rebel forces. Fought in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian]], [[Dantooine system/Legends|Dantooine]], [[Karthakk system|Karthakk]], [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo]], and [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo systems]], each battle saw Phennir and several other Imperial pilots support an Imperial [[Lancer-class frigate/Legends|''Lancer''-class frigate]] as it squared off against a Rebel [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]]. {{Endgame}} [[File:TurrPhennir.png|thumb|right|220px|Turr Phennir during the Galactic Civil War]] In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],{{JournalCite|13|Galaxywide NewsNets}} establishes that the [[raid on Syvris shadowport]] takes place on 38:3:31 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' also places the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]]. Since the [[Battle of Derra IV]], as depicted in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]], takes place between these two events according to ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', it can also be dated to 3 ABY. the pilots of the 181st were briefed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Imperial Fleet]], about an upcoming strike at a Rebel [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that was moving supplies to and from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Derra IV/Legends|Derra IV]]. An Imperial strike force brought Phennir and his fellows to their target, where the 181st participated in an [[Battle of Derra IV|attack]] on an Alliance supply [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] and its starfighter escort. Phennir personally destroyed four [[Transport/Legends|transports]] as the Empire routed its enemies, thereby denying them a critical munitions shipment. After returning to Coruscant, the pilots of the 181st were debriefed, and they were afterward given a lavish ceremony where [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] presented them with medals. For his actions, Phennir was promoted to [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and given control of the fighter group's second squadron, the [[2/181st]]. Derricote was made a [[General/Legends|general]] and promoted out of pilot duty, which left the newly–dubbed [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron]] Fel a colonel and the commanding [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the 181st. Shortly after Derra IV, the 181st were attached to[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 28|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 28]] [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] Vader's personal fleet. When the Empire discovered a hidden Rebel [[Echo Base/Legends|base]] on the remote world of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], Vader immediately brought the fleet to his enemies. [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|attacked]] the base [[Ground warfare/Legends|on the ground]], and Death Squadron remained in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] as Rebel transports evacuated the planet.{{Film|V}} On Vader's orders, the 181st engaged the transports, and, after a great loss of life on both sides, the Rebel base was destroyed. However, a number of Alliance [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were able to escape. ====Battle of Endor==== {{Quote|Isoto acts for the Empire, the Empire we serve.''"
"That'' Empire died with the Emperor. Isoto serves himself, and Pestage and Isard. They are ''not'' the Empire.|Fel and Phennir|}} By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],Phennir's involvement in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] is established in the ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the Battle of Endor in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Phennir had been promoted to major and had been made the 181st's [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]]. The group's starfighters had been upgraded to the [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptor]] following the Battle of Hoth, and Phennir flew the [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] ''[[Saber 2]]'', from where he was responsible for the logistics of the 181st's [[Saber Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Saber Squadron]].{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Saber 2}} His [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] was a pilot with the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[DS-181-4]].{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=DS-181-4}} [[File:TurrPhennirinROTJ.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Phennir flies in the Battle of Endor.]] In 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], the 181st were stationed with Death Squadron''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' at the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]]. The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], who was personally overseeing the final stages of the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] construction, moved the Imperial fleet to the far side of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and lured the Rebel Alliance to the incomplete [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] with the intention of destroying them. When the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attacked]], Death Squadron sprang the trap.{{Film|VI}} Flying in ''Saber 2'', Phennir was assigned to protect the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] and [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] during the ensuing battle, as well as the [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruiser]] [[Pride of Tarlandia/Legends|''Pride of Tarlandia'']]. Despite the 181st's efforts, the latter ship was destroyed by an overwhelming number of Rebel starfighters. Although the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] was protected by a [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]], Rebel [[Endor strike team/Legends|commandos]] on the moon destroyed the [[SLD-26 planetary shield generator/Legends|planetary shield generator]]. Rebel fighters, led by General [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] in the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', then entered the battlestation and raced toward its [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. Phennir was one of several TIE pilots who chased them through the incomplete station. After one of the Rebel ships was destroyed, Calrissian ordered some of the Alliance fighters to split up and head back to the surface, in order to draw off some of the Imperials. Phennir was one of the pilots who broke away to pursue the [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] of Rebel pilot [[Jake Farrell/Legends|Jake Farrell]].{{SW|url=news/death-star-ii-personnel-and-imperial-human-resources|text=Death Star II Personnel: An Imperial Human Resources Guide|nolive=1}} The Rebels were ultimately able to cripple the reactor core, causing the destruction of the entire station. The Imperial fleet fought for several more [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' before retreating to the planet [[Annaj/Legends|Annaj]]. ====Brentaal IV==== {{Quote|He might have power, but it is not the sort that can be transferred to you.''"
"''Sir, if you think I want to supplant…''"
"''I don't think that, Turr. Be cautious, man. There are games being played here, and it's not good for a pawn to dream.|Fel and Phennir discuss Lon Isoto's role in Ysanne Isard's manipulation of events at Brentaal IV|}} The Emperor perished at Endor, and, deprived of their leadership, a number of [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] [[Imperial Civil War|seceded]] from the Empire and declared themselves [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]]. The 181st remained loyal to the Imperial throne, which was seized by [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], but Phennir came to believe that the Empire he had previously served was dead. [[File:PhennirVuultinStarburst-RS23.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Phennir is awarded the Vuultin Starburst.]] The Rebel Alliance reorganized into a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and began to push toward Imperial–held [[Star system/Legends|systems]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], and nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after Endor,''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]'' the 181st were dispatched to defend the planet [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]. The [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|battle]] was orchestrated by [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]],[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 21]] who secretly intended for Brentaal IV to fall and tarnish the image of her [[Politics/Legends|political]] enemies.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 24]] Initially, only the 181st's third squadron was sent, but when they were wiped out and the world's [[Brentaal Moonbase|moonbase]] taken by the New Republic [[New Republic task force (Brentaal IV)|task force]], Phennir and the rest of the fighter group were dispatched to defend the planet itself. After Phennir and his fellows arrived onworld, Fel received orders from [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Lon Isoto]]: protect his capital [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Vuultin]]. Fel was able to persuade him to allow all but one [[Flight group|flight]] of the 181st to instead defend the strategic [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] facilities in the city of [[Oradin]], but Phennir nevertheless came to see Isoto as an incompetent, self–serving coward. The 181st established a base in an Oradin [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], from where they prepared to defend against an imminent New Republic attack. Prior to launching, Phennir sought out Fel and indicated that the entire fighter group would follow him if he disregarded all of Isoto's commands. He further suggested that Fel usurp Isoto and become a warlord based out of Brentaal IV. Although sympathetic, Fel dismissed the suggestion and remained committed to defending the planet's citizens. Over Oradin, the 181st resisted an attack by the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing fighter]] group [[Aggressor Wing]], and Fel led [[Alpha Squadron (TIE/IN interceptor)/Legends|Alpha Squadron]] in destroying them while Phennir took the remainder of the fighter group to make a run on attacking [[Bantha-class troop carrier assault shuttle|''Bantha''-class troop carrier assault shuttle|assault carriers]]. Phennir singlehandedly brought one of the [[Carrier/Legends|carriers]] down. When Isoto [[Comlink/Legends|commed]] Fel and demanded that he retreat to the besieged Vuultin, Phennir urged him to ignore the command, but Fel relented to his superior officer and took Alpha Squadron to defend the capital. He ordered Phennir to leave nothing for Oradin's conquerors, and the Major inflicted heavy damage on the city before following the [[Baron/Legends|Baron]]. However, the attack at Vuultin had been a feint, and the New Republic forces retreated to a defenseless Oradin. ====The fall of Vuultin==== {{Quote|Our people here shot down Rogues. Granted, they got the better of the exchange, but 3rd squadron was understrength and our least experienced. Tomorrow, or whenever they come, we will destroy them.|Turr Phennir|}} In Vuultin, the members of the 181st were invited into the throne room of Isoto's lavish [[Lon Isoto's palace|palace]], where the Admiral celebrated the city's survival and presented Phennir with an award for his destruction of the carrier: The [[Vuultin Starburst]], created specially for the occasion by Isoto himself. Save for Fel, who refused any reward, the rest of the fighter group received [[Oradin Diamond]] awards, which Isoto also gave to himself. Despite Fel's humility, Isoto declared the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] "[[Fel's Day]]," and a round of [[Wine/Legends|wine]] drinking ensued. Phennir spoke with Fel and dismissed his medal, believing that the battle had been just another day to kill their enemies. Fel suggested that Isoto's overconfidence was affecting Phennir, and opined to the shocked Major that the entire incident was a game orchestrated by higher powers. At that moment, [[Grania (Brentaali)|Grania]]—one of Isoto's concubines—approached the two and offered herself to them. Phennir followed Fel's lead in politely refusing, but his reluctance showed to the Baron, who gave him the go–ahead. Phennir and Grania then proceeded to a more private location. [[File:Phennirvstycho.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Phennir pursues Tycho Celchu over Brentaal IV.]] Not long afterward, the New Republic struck at Vuultin. As the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighters]] of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] assaulted the city's defenses, the 181st launched to engage them, and the battle soon moved from [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] into space. Several interceptors were shot down by the Rogues while the rest of the 181st attacked the Y-wings of Aggressor Wing. Phennir approached Rogue pilot [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] and attacked with a determination to kill one of the New Republic's best pilots; however, Vuultin had fallen, and the battle was already lost. Fel thus allowed Phennir only one pass at Celchu before taking the 181st to regroup with the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Eviscerator/Legends|''Eviscerator'']]. When Celchu reversed his throttle and slipped behind Phennir, the Major broke to port and retreated to the ''Eviscerator''. Meanwhile, Fel's interceptor was crippled by an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion blast]] from Aggressor Wing's Colonel [[Horton Salm/Legends|Horton Salm]]. Captured by the New Republic, Fel defected from an Empire in which he too had stopped believing, and command of the 181st was given to Phennir.''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' ===Imperial ace=== ====New Republic expansion==== {{Quote|The real One Eighty–first is still serving the Empire with loyalty and skill, under Turr Phennir.|Teren Rogriss, in 7 ABY|}} The New Republic continued their coreward push toward Coruscant over the next two years, and in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Phennir and the 181st were stationed at the [[Bilbringi Shipyards]], where they were answerable to Admiral [[Teren Rogriss]]. When the New Republic's Admiral [[Nammo]] brought the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Home One'' type Star Cruiser]] [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']] to [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]], Phennir and the 181st aided Rogriss in [[Battle of Bilbringi (6 ABY)|defeating]] their foe. The New Republic nevertheless [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|conquered]] Coruscant in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], and Imperial leadership was thrown into flux. Various individuals ruled over the next few years,''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and Phennir and the 181st remained loyal to the throne. When [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] [[Thrawn campaign|defended]] Bilbringi from the New Republic in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], the 181st flew in the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|battle]], but the [[Thrawn's fleet (Galactic Empire)|Imperial fleet]] fled when Thrawn was betrayed and killed by his [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]]. In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the entire [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] was [[Battle of Ord Cantrell|killed or captured]], and the Galactic Empire ceased to exist. The holdings of various warlords were [[Imperial Reunification|unified]] to form the [[Imperial Remnant]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]; by that time, Phennir—now considered the Empire's greatest starfighter [[Ace/Legends|ace]]—was stationed with the 181st on the planet [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]], in Remnant territory. ====Regaining ground==== [[File:Acephennir.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Turr Phennir in a celebration]] Remnant Supreme Commander [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] sought to expand the new government's borders through conquest, and he brought the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']] to Borosk to collect Phennir and the 181st. The fighter group and the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] initiated their [[Orinda campaign]] by swiftly [[Raid on Orinda|capturing]] the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Orinda system/Legends|Orinda system]] and six coreward systems along the [[Entralla Route/Legends|Entralla Route]]. Their final [[Battle of Lonnaw|victory]] was in the [[Lonnaw system/Legends|Lonnaw system]], from where Pellaeon dispatched emissaries to the New Republic, informing them that no further offensives would be launched if the new Imperial borders were respected. Instead, the New Republic dispatched to the front lines a fleet led by General Wedge Antilles that consisted of Rogue Squadron and the ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]''. Before Antilles caught up with Pellaeon's fleet, the 181st and a complement of [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] [[Battle of Hewett|captured]] [[Battle of Tartaglia|the]] defenseless systems of [[Hewett system/Legends|Hewett]] and [[Tartaglia system/Legends|Tartaglia]]. When Antilles arrived in the Mid Rim, Phennir brought the 181st to the ''Reaper''{{'s}} aid as the fleet engaged Rogue Squadron and the ''Lusankya'' at the [[Darkon system/Legends|Darkon]] and [[Traval-Pacor system/Legends|Traval-Pacor systems]]. [[Battle of Darkon|Both]] [[Battle of the Traval-Pacor system|engagements]] were won by the New Republic, who secured the systems. The 181st and the Rogues then [[Battle of Orocco|fought]] to a stalemate in the [[Orocco system/Legends|Orocco system]]. Afterward, the 181st and the ''Lusankya'' [[Battle of Tyan|battled]] for control of the [[Vvan system|Tyan system]], and the 181st emerged victorious. At that point, the [[Endurance-class fleet carrier|fleet carrier]] [[Endurance (Endurance-class)|''Endurance'']] was added to the New Republic's arsenal, and it joined Antilles's fleet in [[Battle of Obredaan|defeating]] the 181st and the ''Reaper'' in the [[Obredaan system/Legends|Obredaan system]]. Pellaeon's fleet fell back to Orinda, and Phennir led the 181st as, alongside the ''Reaper'', it [[Battle of Orinda|defended]] the world against the entirety of Antilles's forces. Pellaeon sprung a trap when six [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|interdictor cruisers]] and the Star Dreadnought [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] decanted from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]; the ''Endurance'' was destroyed, and the ''Lusankya'' retreated with the Rogues. Orinda was secured as the edge of the Imperial Remnant's territory, but subsequent border clashes with the New Republic became common. ====Emissary of the Empire==== {{Quote|General Turr Phennir.''"
"''Wedge Antilles.''"
"''It seems we're here for the same reason.''"
"''I suspect so.''"
"''You'll understand if I don't wish you luck.''"
"''Likewise.|Wedge Antilles and Phennir, after meeting on Adumar|}} [[File:Finewine.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Phennir drinking wine]] In [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' both the Imperial Remnant and New Republic turned their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] to the neutral planet [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]]. Located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], Adumar was a planet with rich ordnance–producing resources that had just recently discovered the [[The galaxy/Legends|wider galaxy]]. Its citizens held fighter pilots in high esteem, and when the Imperial Remnant discovered that it was interested in joining them, Phennir—now a general—and three other members of the 181st were dispatched on a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission to a world that insisted on negotiating with the Remnant's best pilots. Admiral Rogriss brought them to Adumar in the Star Destroyer [[Agonizer (starship)|''Agonizer'']], and, after making planetfall in the nation state of [[Cartann]], the pilots were welcomed by the nation's government with pomp and ceremony. That evening, they attended a reception at the [[Perator's palace|palace]] of Cartann's [[perator]], [[Pekaelic ke Teldan]], in the nation's capital of [[Cartann City]]. After leaving their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] at the door, Phennir and his men were greeted by a large crowd of government officials and pilots in the palace's outer hall. Phennir was surprised to bump into Wedge Antilles, and, after a reach for his blaster came up empty, he realized that Adumar had also invited the New Republic's best pilots—Antilles, Celchu, [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]], and [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]]—in an effort to judge both governments. In a tense moment, Phennir and Antilles [[Handshake|shook hands]], acknowledged each other's missions, and parted company. Before the night was over, the pilots bore witness to a culturally significant duel between two locals, [[Cheriss ke Hanadi]] and [[Depird ke Fanax]], who fought with Adumar's local [[blastsword]] weapon. ====Unfriendly competition==== {{Quote|The Imperial pilots show more affection for Cartann by doing things the Cartann way; how can the people of Cartann not respond with more affection?''"
"''By remembering the sons and daughters they've lost?|Cheriss ke Hanadi and Wedge Antilles, on Phennir's methods|}} With Phennir and Antilles now aware of each other's presence, Rogriss was made to swear to Cartann's government that he would leave the [[Adumar system/Legends|system]] peacefully should they choose to side with the New Republic. Over the next several days, Phennir and his men frequently tested out Adumar's [[Blade-32 superiority fighter]]s in the planet's skies. After launching from the [[Cartann Bladedrome]] military base, they were routinely challenged to dogfights by local pilots, who sought to gain honor by killing them in accordance with local customs. They accepted and won each challenge, killing their opponents and gaining considerable honor themselves in the eyes of their hosts. [[File:PhennirTIE-RS24.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Phennir's TIE interceptor]] Their popularity in Cartann rose above that of Antilles and his three Rogue Squadron pilots, who exclusively used simulated weapons in their aerial duels. Phennir and his men were often invited to dinner by Cartann [[Noble/Legends|nobles]], and as the days passed, the invitations began to come from nobles of higher prestige, who preferred Phennir's adherence to local customs over Antilles's effort to spare lives. One day, the 181st pilots accepted challenges from and shot down two half [[Flightknife|squadrons]] of experienced pilots. While returning from the base to the city, their wheeled transport was surrounded by gawking locals, who shouted accolades and passed them gifts. The procession stalled Antilles' own transport, and Phennir gave his opposite number a mocking smile. With the prospect of joining the [[galactic community]] becoming very real, Cartann's government invited representatives from the world's other nations to Cartann City in order to discuss the possibility of forming a unified planetary government. A reception was held at the Perator's palace, and Phennir and his men attended in Adumari dress. Phennir kept a watchful eye on Antilles' men, who stood out in New Republic dress uniforms, while the other 181st pilots regaled admiring nobles with dogfighting anecdotes. Before the Perator spoke, ke Hanadi accepted and won two consecutive challenges from blastsword duelists, after which Perator ke Teldan gave a speech in front of a [[flatcam]] that broadcast across all of Adumar. He declared that Cartann would henceforth be the center of an Adumari government led by himself, and when the [[flatscreen]]s turned off, he told the visiting dignitaries that they had one day to officially acquiesce. Two more blastsword duels closed off the night, and the following evening, Phennir and his men followed the New Republic pilots' lead and arrived at the Perator's palace in formal Imperial attire. Ke Teldan announced that the [[Yedagon Confederacy]] and the nation of [[Halbegardia]] had issued statements of defiance, and he asked the eight visiting pilots if they would fly with Cartann in a [[Adumari Civil War|war]] against the two nations. Phennir immediately pledged his men to the Perator's cause, but Antilles refused, and the New Republic pilots were consequently [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from Cartann. ====Fighting for Cartann==== {{Quote|Would you—and it would please us greatly if you would—lead units of the Cartann armed forces in action against our enemies, so that we might grasp the full measure of your skill and honor?''"
"''It would be my tremendous honor to demonstrate what we have to offer the people of Cartann and Adumar.|Perator ke Teldan and Phennir|}} [[File:Saber2-CCGDS2.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Phennir's interceptor in flight]] The 181st pilots helped Perator ke Teldan prepare for war. A surprise attack on Cartann came just before dawn two days later, as Antilles and his pilots led the forces of Yedagon and its allies into battle. Phennir and his men launched in their interceptors and joined the [[Blade series|Blade fighters]] that fought to defend the nation's capital. They engaged the [[Adumari Union]] above a [[Tree/Legends|forest]] canopy several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] out from Cartann City, and [[Unidentified 181st pilot|one of]] Phennir's men was killed when Antilles and his Rogues concentrated their Blades' [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire on his interceptor. The remaining three Imperial pilots subsequently disabled Celchu's craft but were forced to scatter from the guns of a [[Meteor-class Aerial Fort|''Meteor''-class Aerial Fort]]. The Union advance continued, but Phennir and his men destroyed large numbers of enemy Blades and quickly retreated at unpredictable angles whenever approached by a large formation. The battle eventually came to the city itself, and the New Republic pilots retrieved their captured X-wing fighters from the Perator's palace. While the Imperials were picking off targets in the skies above the city's west end, Antilles arrived in his X-wing and killed [[Unidentified 181st pilot 2|another of]] Phennir's men. Phennir and his [[Unidentified 181st pilot 3|remaining pilot]] damaged Klivian's fighter before pursuing Antilles and Celchu, but a laser salvo from Antilles reduced their number to just Phennir. Outgunned, he rocketed skyward and returned to the ''Agonizer''. Without the skill of their Imperial allies, the Cartann defense fell apart, and they surrendered. Admiral Rogriss joined Antilles on the ground, having lost faith in an Empire he knew would force him to break his word and punish the Adumari for siding with the New Republic. As the ''Agonizer'' left the system, its crew found itself unable to contact the Imperial Remnant due to a [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] Rogriss had locked down. After their return to Imperial space, Phennir gathered the rest of the 181st and joined a task force that was dispatched to Adumar to [[Battle of Adumar|attack]]. Falsified local news reports had suggested that Adumar was still at war, but the task force instead found several New Republic [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] orbiting a unified world. Phennir nevertheless led elements of the 181st out of a Star Destroyer's hangar bay, escorting numerous [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]] squadrons into Cartann airspace. Phennir and several others piloted advanced [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defenders]]. The group was met by New Republic and Adumari fighter squadrons, and a series of dogfights began. Phennir met Antilles in a head–to–head pass and scored a hit, which damaged his enemy's X-wing. Antilles got the better of the next pass, destroying one of the solar wing arrays of Phennir's Defender. With his fighter venting atmosphere, Phennir retreated to the ''Agonizer'', which had suffered heavy damage. The task force fled the system shortly thereafter, leaving Adumar to the New Republic. ===Warfare renewed=== ====Re-emergence==== {{Quote|We haven't been introduced.''"
"''General Turr Phennir. Supreme Commander of the Confederation military. At your service.''"
"''I thought…''"
"''That today would be the day that office was elected. A deception I thought would be useful in drawing your forces in.|Jacen Solo speaks to a hologram of Phennir during the Battle of Gilatter VIII|}} [[File:Battle_of_Gilatter_VIII.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Phennir's hologram observes a lightsaber duel aboard the Gilatter VIII resort satellite.]] After Adumar, Phennir remained out of action for many years.{{CSWECite|III|23|Phennir, Turr}} [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] saw the Imperial Remnant sign a [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|peace treaty]] with the New Republic, bringing the Galactic Civil War to an end. The New Republic reorganized into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], and in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], tensions arose between the Alliance and the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' The Alliance placed [[Sector Defense Limits|limitations]] on the military capabilities of its member worlds, and the independently–minded [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] challenged the restrictions while secretly building a [[Corellian Home Fleet|fleet]]. As the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] broke out between the Galactic Alliance and the Corellian system's [[Five Worlds]] leadership, other planetary governments flocked to the Corellians' cause, including those of [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]], [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], Adumar and [[Atzerri/Legends|Atzerri]]. The newly–formed [[Confederation]] sought a wartime leader, and Phennir resurfaced to become a general and the [[Supreme Commander of the Confederation military]], the highest position in the organization.''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'' In his new office, he sought to lead the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet]] into a trap, and a ruse was developed to draw the enemy to the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Gilatter VIII]]. Knowledge of Phennir's appointment was kept top secret. Instead, information was leaked to [[Galactic Alliance Intelligence Service|Galactic Alliance Intelligence]] that an upcoming face–to–face meeting of Confederate delegates at an abandoned [[Gilatter VIII resort satellite|resort satellite]] orbiting Gilatter VIII would elect a Supreme Commander, who would then lead a fleet action against an Alliance target. [[Confederation Fleet|Confederate forces]] spent several days performing minor raids on Alliance [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], and Alliance Intelligence concluded that a major shipyard offensive would follow the meeting. In reality, [[Actor/Legends|actors]] were sent to the [[resort satellite]] in the guise of government officials, and the surrounding space was filled with [[Mine/Legends|mines]]. Confederate capital ships waited in nearby space, and on the day of the false meeting, Phennir projected his presence into the [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]]. After directing the actors' attention to the arrival of Alliance forces, visible through the [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]], he acknowledged the presence of Alliance Colonel [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], whom Phennir had learned had infiltrated the meeting. He then personally mocked Solo for falling for the trap, but the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Colonel was able to [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] warn his allies about the minefield before escaping from the resort. In the ensuing chaos, Phennir's hologram observed a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] aboard the resort satellite that pitted [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his sister [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] against the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] [[Lumiya]] and the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alema Rar]]. It ended in a stalemate. A concurrent [[Battle of Gilatter VIII|space battle]] between the Confederation and the Alliance was an even slugging match, and both sides retreated after taking moderate losses. The Confederation celebrated the engagement as a victory for Phennir, and their ranks were bolstered by new governments, including that of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]. Shortly thereafter, Confederate forces began to push toward the enemy's capital world of Coruscant. [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] and Commenorian fleets [[Second Battle of Balmorra|captured]] [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]], and a joint Corellian–[[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] force [[Battle of Kuat (Second Galactic Civil War)|fought]] a [[Standard week/Legends|week]]–long struggle against the Galactic Alliance at [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]].''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'' ====War and politics==== {{Quote|I don't say this often, Koyan, but I'll say it now. You're an idiot.''"
"''Which makes you an even more exceptional idiot, for saying it to the man with the most destructive weapon ever created.''"
"''As you have the most destructive weapon ever created, you are clearly capable of defending the Corellian system without aid from the rest of the Confederation. No need for synchronized fleet movements. For sharing intelligence with the other worlds. For food, medicines, supplies.|Phennir and Sadras Koyan|''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''}} The Confederation abandoned Kuat in order to aid the neutral planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] when it was [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|bombarded]] by Jacen Solo—now the Alliance [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]]—who accused the native [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] of harboring a group of Jedi that had withdrawn from the Alliance due to Solo's increasingly brutal war tactics. Solo's [[Fifth Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Galactic Alliance Fifth Fleet]] fled from the combined forces of the Confederation, the [[Wookiee fleet]], the [[New Jedi Order]], and the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]. Following the battle, the latter three groups formed the [[Jedi Coalition]] in opposition to Solo, and the Confederation agreed not to interfere in their affairs. However, Phennir's coreward push had ground to a halt. [[File:Phennirprofile.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Turr Phennir celebrating one of his triumphs]] Following the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]] of Kashyyyk, Confederation vessels remained on the world and helped extinguish the blazing forest [[Fire/Legends|fires]]. Around that time, Phennir learned that the Corellians were arranging a secret meeting with Solo; when he inquired as to their intentions, he was told that they were attempting to keep him busy for a few days. In truth, [[Five World Prime Minister]] [[Sadras Koyan]] had reactivated the derelict [[Centerpoint Station]], an ancient superweapon that lay in the Corellian system, and when Solo arrived at a deep–space rendezvous point to negotiate, Centerpoint's powerful [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsor]] weapon was [[Battle of the rendezvous point|fired]] at his [[Second Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|fleet]]. Solo's [[Anakin Solo (starship)|flagship]] escaped unscathed, and a furious Phennir contacted Koyan to dress him down for independently utilizing a Confederate resource and for alerting the Alliance that a strategic weapon was once again operational. When Koyan remained belligerent, Phennir cut Corellia off from all Confederate aid, including medicine, supplies, intelligence, and synchronized fleet movements. Not long afterward, Phennir was contacted by [[Denjax Teppler]], Corellia's [[Minister of Information (Five Worlds)|Minister of Information]], who appealed to the General to end the embargo. Phennir compared Koyan to a [[Poison/Legends|poison]] that would spread through the body of the Confederation unless its [[Limb/Legends|arm]]—Corellia—were amputated. He would only honor Teppler's request if the poison were removed. When Centerpoint Station was later [[Battle of Centerpoint Station (Second Galactic Civil War)|attacked]] by both the Galactic Alliance and the Jedi Coalition, Phennir was again contacted by Teppler, who asked for Confederate reinforcements and promised that Koyan would soon cease to be a problem. When Phennir received confirmation that Koyan was no longer in power, he dispatched [[Warship/Legends|warships]] to aid the suffering Corellian fleet. During the battle, a Jedi strike team boarded and sabotaged Centerpoint Station, which subsequently fired on and destroyed itself, along with most of the Confederate and Alliance vessels in the system. ====Endgame==== {{Quote|I'll admit that I don't understand you Corellians. You place the value of freedom so far above that of duty that you're incomprehensible to me. I've flown with and against the best, most disciplined pilots Corellia has offered—Soontir Fel, Wedge Antilles—and I don't even understand ''them''. Perhaps that's my failing, but the Confederation will fall apart if Koyan remains in charge. Get me someone who can understand ''me''.|Turr Phennir|}} As the war raged into [[41 ABY]], a Galactic Alliance [[Second Battle of Fondor|attack]] on the [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|Fondor Shipyards]] turned the tide of the conflict. Although the Imperial Remnant entered the war on the Alliance's side, Solo's co–Chief of State [[Cha Niathal]] broke away from the government and formed an [[Alliance-in-exile]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' that later joined the Jedi Coalition, as did [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] forces and the [[Maw Irregular Fleet]] of the rogue warlord [[Natasi Daala]]. A subsequent [[Second Battle of Roche|clash]] in the [[Roche system]] saw the Confederation suffer a considerable loss when Bothan and Corellian fleets were trapped by Alliance Admiral [[Nek Bwua'tu]]. The war's final [[Battle of Uroro Station|battle]] saw Solo killed by his [[Twin/Legends|twin sister]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] in a lightsaber duel, which prompted the entire Jedi Coalition to join forces with the Galactic Alliance and the Imperial Remnant in an effort to force the Confederation to the bargaining table. The Confederation surrendered to the combined might of its enemies, and the war was brought to an end.''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' However, the organization remained independent from the Alliance, and Phennir continued to serve as their leader during the war's fallout. Midway through [[43 ABY]], Phennir was invited to Coruscant to attend a [[Unification Summit]]. Hosted by the Galactic Alliance, its aim was to explore the relationships between the Alliance, Confederation, and Imperial Remnant, and to ultimately restore a strong planetary union. On the galactic capital world, Phennir joined Alliance Chief of State Natasi Daala and Imperial [[Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|Head of State]] [[Jagged Fel]] at the head table of a large chamber within the world's [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Tiurrg Drey'lye]] of the [[Senate Unification Preparations Committee]] chaired the opening session of the summit, where speeches were made by various parties. Following the initial session, the summit continued over the next several weeks, but in [[44 ABY]], Daala was [[Plan Delta|deposed]] and arrested,''[[Fate of the Jedi: Conviction]]'' and Fel left Coruscant to deal with Imperial affairs.''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|What's his record for today?''"
"''He accepted two challenges, and he and his pilots shot down two half flightknives. Experienced pilots, good ones. He gained considerable honor today.''"
"''Yes, he's just rolling in the honor coupons. They stick to the blood all over him.|Wedge Antilles and Cheriss ke Hanadi discuss Phennir's actions on Adumar|}} [[File:Conspiringpilots.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Phennir urges Soontir Fel to rebel against Admiral Isoto.]] During his early Imperial service, Turr Phennir was dedicated to furthering the glory of the Empire, and, despite his aristocratic background, cared not for rank or family prestige, but how many enemies of the Empire he got to shoot down. Those beliefs also made him turn down an offer to join Black Squadron when he joined the 181st. However, he lost faith in Imperial leadership following the Battle of Endor, believing that the Empire had died with its Emperor. Phennir doubted the competence of Admiral Lon Isoto and repeatedly urged Soontir Fel to refuse Isoto's orders at Brentaal IV, opining that ignoring the commands of an incompetent superior was outside the boundaries of mutiny. He suggested that the Baron instead seize control of the world and declare himself a warlord; ambitious, Phennir hoped to serve as Fel's second–in–command. However, he deferred to Fel's decision to follow Isoto's orders and protect Brentaal IV's citizens. Despite his lack of faith in the Empire, Phennir continued to serve for a further nine years. Phennir was eager for glory, and he briefly strayed from his mission profile on Brentaal IV to challenge Tycho Celchu, one of the New Republic's best pilots. Despite his ambition, Phennir was indifferent toward the medal that he received from Isoto, maintaining the views from his earlier Imperial service that his actions during the battle had been just another opportunity to kill his enemies. He had no moral quandaries with killing, and he eagerly and frequently participated in Cartann's customary duels–to–the–death while on Adumar. His actions and his respect for Adumari customs gained him considerable honor with the locals, which Phennir was quick to smugly flaunt to Wedge Antilles, his opposite number. Phennir had a low opinion of New Republic forces and was furious whenever Antilles upstaged him in front of their Adumari hosts. Confident that it would lead to Adumar joining the Empire, he was quick to pledge himself and his men to Cartann's war effort when the nation's Perator declared his intention to conquer the planet by force. Phennir's reputation during his Imperial career was one of combat savagery and backstabbing politics, but by 43 ABY, it had evolved into one of honorable service and pragmatism. Phennir disdained politicians, and during his service as Supreme Commander of the Confederation military, he openly insulted both Sadras Koyan and Denjax Teppler to their faces. He berated the former for using Centerpoint Station without his blessing, and he cut the entire Corellian system off from Confederate aid due to what he saw as gross incompetenece on Koyan's part. However, Teppler won both an apology and a measure of Phennir's respect when he showed candor while requesting an end to the embargo. Phennir believed that Koyan's Corellian spirit of freedom contributed to his "mental deficits," and he insisted that Koyan be removed from power by any means necessary, else the entire Confederation would fall apart. Phennir stood below average height and sported fair, [[Color/Legends|blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that seemed naturally unruly despite being cut into a military style. With dark brown eyes that suggested cutting intelligence, light–colored skin, high cheekbones, and aristocratic, lean features, he was handsome but for a scar that curved across his left cheek, but his overall appearance lacked warmth. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|All of you are heroes, of course, but one of you is more. Singlehandedly, he brought down an assault shuttle. Major Turr Phennir, step forward. To you, sir, goes the first ever Vuultin Starburst. I designed it myself to reward your valor.''"
"''I am honored, sir.''"
"''Of course you are, Major.|Lon Isoto and Turr Phennir|}} [[File:PhennirFel-RS23.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Turr Phennir and Soontir Fel on Brentaal IV]] Throughout his years as a pilot, Phennir enjoyed a celebrated career. He personally destroyed four Rebel transports at Derra IV and singlehandedly brought down a New Republic assault carrier at Brentaal IV. During the Orinda campaign, he led the 181st to victory over the ''Lusankya'', an ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought. In 12 ABY, he was considered the Empire's greatest starfighter ace, and by the following year, the most famous surviving Imperial pilot. However, Wedge Antilles bested him in a dogfight over Adumar in that year. Phennir served as the 181st's executive officer, a post in which he was responsible for squadron logistics. After Fel's defection, he was promoted to command of the group and led them to several victories over the following years. As the leader of the Confederation military, he showed prowess as a tactician, successfully luring the Galactic Alliance into a trap at Gilatter VIII. ==Equipment== [[File:Turr_Phennir_XWM.png|thumb|right|250px|Phennir's TIE interceptor was adorned with bloodstripes.]] During his career as an Imperial pilot, Phennir was equipped with the standard Imperial TIE pilot [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] and [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] while in flight, and otherwise wore an [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]], complete with [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia plaque]]. During his career, Phennir operated a variety of Imperial starfighters, including the TIE fighter, TIE interceptor, and TIE Defender. Phennir's fighters typically bore [[Bloodstripe (symbol)|red stripes]] on the wings, representing a minimum of ten kills.{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Saber 1}} His flight suit and uniform were also decorated with red stripes. Phennir wore a [[Holster/Legends|holster]] capable of carrying a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. When first appointed as Supreme Commander of the Confederation military, Phennir sported a white admiral's uniform in a style reminiscent of the early days of the Empire. He was also known to wear the dark uniform of a Confederation general during this time in his career. ==Behind the scenes== Turr Phennir was created by author [[Michael A. Stackpole]] as a character in the [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''X-wing Rogue Squadron'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], and he first appeared in the series' [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|twenty–second issue]], which was penciled by [[John Nadeau]] and published in [[September]] [[1997]]. He also appeared in the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|next]] [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|three]] [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|issues]], the two former of which were penciled by Nadeau and the latter by [[Steve Crespo]]. All four issues were written by Stackpole and were part of the larger "[[Rise of Isard]]" story arc that was developed by Stackpole and editor [[Peet Janes]].{{WebCite|url=http://starwarsfanworks.com/chronoradio.html|text=Special Guest: Michael Stackpole|work=Chronoradio|archivedate=20110622070138}} One of Phennir's cards in ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'', a [[2000]] expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]], uses a screenshot of a TIE pilot from [[1983]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] to represent Phennir; while the pilot flies a TIE fighter in the film, Phennir has been established as flying a TIE interceptor during the battle. In the [[2015]] [[StarWars.com]] article "[[Death Star II Personnel: An Imperial Human Resources Guide]]," [[Tim Veekhoven]] used a screenshot of the same pilot to represent Phennir. In the same article, Veekhoven established Phennir as the pilot of the lone TIE interceptor that is seen pursuing Jake Farrell's A-wing out of the Death Star in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. Phennir was also featured in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' in [[2012]], though not all content written about him made the final cut. In [[2014]], the StarWars.com article "[[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]]" released some of the cut content related to Phennir along with other material cut from the book. Author [[Paul R. Urquhart]] further went on to mention that his endnotes stated that Phennir was the child of [[Omi Vane|Omi]] and [[Tofen Vane]],{{JCFCite|url=posts/51833447|board=Literature|thread=The Official StarWars.com Blog Thread - That's right, I went there|user=[[Paul R. Urquhart|Thrawn McEwok]]|date=2014-09-11|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150926053710/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-starwars-com-blog-thread-thats-right-i-went-there.50015309/page-49}} who were introduced in the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Hero of the Confederacy|''Hero of the Confederacy'']] story arc of the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (comic series)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'']] comic-book series.[[The Clone Wars 10|''The Clone Wars'' 10]] In the comic, Tofen dies fighting for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].[[The Clone Wars 12|''The Clone Wars'' 12]] Urquhart stated that Omi Vane remarried Count Phennir, a nobleman from [[Eiattu system/Legends|Eiattu IV]] and Turr simply adopted the "Phennir" surname to avoid raising too many questions. Although published sources only refer to Cartha as Turr Phennir's brother, Urquhart stated that Cartha was intended to be Count Phennir's son and Turr's stepbrother. ==Appearances== Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]] |years=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]According to Phennir's entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', he took command of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]] following [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel's]] defection to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Fel's defection is depicted in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]], which takes place approximately ten [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] according to [[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3|''Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' Volume 3]]. Since ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the Battle of Endor early in 39:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the start of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]], Fel's defection also occurs in 4 ABY.—c.
